title: quercus brantii var. oophora
intro: Quercus brantii var. oophora, also known as Persian oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Afghanistan. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to exhibit medicinal properties in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus brantii var. oophora include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are typically prepared by steeping the bark, leaves, or roots in hot water to release their active compounds.
